title: SecretError::Unavailable misused for 'not supported' operations
description: bitwarden::put, aws-kms::get/put, and wolfhsm::get/put all return Unavailable with message 'X is not supported'. Unavailable semantically means 'transient failure, retry may succeed'. Callers implementing retry/fallback logic will retry operations that will never succeed. A caller using bitwarden or aws-kms who writes a retry loop around put() will spin forever. The right error is Backend (permanent failure) or a future Unsupported variant. Since SecretError is #[non_exhaustive], adding Unsupported is non-breaking in the next minor version. The pattern will be copied by future contributors adding new backends.
